title: Database Connection Timeout / DB Unreachable
symptoms: Application returns 500 errors or 'Service Unavailable' for database-dependent operations
Connection pool exhaustion alerts from the application tier
Database query response time exceeds SLA threshold (>5 seconds)
Database server CPU or I/O at 100%
steps: Check if the database service is running: 'systemctl status mysql/postgresql/oracle' on the DB server
Check DB server connectivity: ping and telnet to DB port (3306 MySQL, 5432 PostgreSQL, 1521 Oracle)
Review DB server resource utilization: CPU, memory, disk I/O — identify the bottleneck
Check slow query log: identify long-running queries causing lock contention
Check active connections: 'SHOW PROCESSLIST' (MySQL) or 'SELECT * FROM pg_stat_activity' (PG)
Check disk space on the DB volume — a full disk will crash the DB immediately
Review application connection pool settings — maxPoolSize may be too low for current load
Check if recent schema migration or code deployment changed query patterns
resolution: If DB is down: restart the service and verify data integrity. If disk full: free space immediately (rotate logs, move backups). If slow queries: kill long-running transactions, optimize indexes. If connection pool exhausted: increase pool size temporarily and investigate application query volume. Notify application team and DBA.
escalation: If the database cannot be restarted or data corruption is suspected, page the DBA on-call immediately. If a customer-facing billing or CRM database is affected, escalate to both DBA and Application Manager.
estimated_resolution_time: Service restart: 5-10 minutes. Slow query fix: 15-30 minutes. Disk emergency: 10-20 minutes.
